```json
  "findings": [
    "Shaft alignment performed without accounting for thermal growth offset on hot service equipment",
    "Operating misalignment expected as machine heats from cold alignment state to running temperature",
    "Likely cause of elevated vibration, coupling wear, bearing overheating, and seal degradation"
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Stop or schedule shutdown to perform hot alignment check or reverse-calculate thermal offset",
    "Apply OEM/API 686 thermal growth values and realign machine at ambient with target offset compensation",
    "Verify final alignment with laser system after machine reaches steady-state operating temperature"
  ],
  "rationale": "The observed condition is a non-compliant installation: shaft alignment was set cold without compensating for documented thermal growth. On hot service machinery, this causes the running alignment to shift out of tolerance, producing a degraded in-service condition. API 686 requires alignment procedures to account for thermal movement and piping strain; ignoring thermal growth violates this and creates high risk of accelerated wear, seal failure, and unplanned shutdown.",
  "references": [
    "API 686"
  ]
}
```
